children.label: A
children.text: involve individuals with disabilities or parents of individuals with disabilities ages birth through 26 in planning, implementing, and evaluating the project; and

children.label: B
children.text: where appropriate, determine whether the project has any potential for replication and adoption by other entities.

children.label: A
children.text: share in the cost of the project;

children.label: B
children.text: prepare any findings and products from the project in formats that are useful for specific audiences, including parents, administrators, teachers, early intervention personnel, related services personnel, and individuals with disabilities;

children.label: C
children.text: disseminate such findings and products; and

children.label: D
children.text: collaborate with other such recipients in carrying out subparagraphs (B) and (C).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and use a standing panel of experts who are qualified, by virtue of their training, expertise, or experience, to evaluate each application under part B or C that requests more than $75,000 per year in Federal financial assistance.

children.label: i
children.text: individuals who are representatives of institutions of higher education that plan, develop, and carry out high quality programs of personnel preparation;

children.label: ii
children.text: individuals who design and carry out scientifically based research targeted to the improvement of special education programs and services;

children.label: iii
children.text: individuals who have recognized experience and knowledge necessary to integrate and apply scientifically based research findings to improve educational and transitional results for children with disabilities;

children.label: iv
children.text: individuals who administer programs at the State or local level in which children with disabilities participate;

children.label: v
children.text: individuals who prepare parents of children with disabilities to participate in making decisions about the education of their children;

children.label: vi
children.text: individuals who establish policies that affect the delivery of services to children with disabilities;

children.label: vii
children.text: individuals who are parents of children with disabilities ages birth through 26 who are benefiting, or have benefited, from coordinated research, personnel preparation, and technical assistance; and

children.label: viii
children.text: individuals with disabilities.

children.heading: Term
children.label: C Term
children.text: No individual shall serve on the standing panel for more than 3 consecutive years.

children.label: i
children.text: individuals with knowledge and expertise on the issues addressed by the activities described in the application; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent practicable, parents of children with disabilities ages birth through 26, individuals with disabilities, and persons from diverse backgrounds.

children.heading: Federal employment limitation
children.label: B Federal employment limitation
children.text: A majority of the individuals on each subpanel that reviews an application under part B or C shall be individuals who are not employees of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Use of discretionary funds for administrative purposes
children.label: 3 Use of discretionary funds for administrative purposes

children.heading: Expenses and fees of non-Federal panel members
children.label: A Expenses and fees of non-Federal panel members
children.text: The Secretary may use funds available under part B or C to pay the expenses and fees of the panel members who are not officers or employees of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Administrative support
children.label: B Administrative support
children.text: The Secretary may use not more than 1 percent of the funds appropriated to carry out part B or C to pay non-Federal entities for administrative support related to management of applications submitted under part B or C, respectively.

children.heading: Program evaluation
children.label: c Program evaluation
children.text: The Secretary may use funds made available to carry out part B or C to evaluate activities carried out under part B or C, respectively.

children.label: A
children.text: $12,832,000 to address the educational, related services, transitional, and early intervention needs of children with deaf-blindness.

children.label: B
children.text: $4,000,000 to address the postsecondary, vocational, technical, continuing, and adult education needs of individuals with deafness.

children.label: C
children.text: $4,000,000 to address the educational, related services, and transitional needs of children with an emotional disturbance and those who are at risk of developing an emotional disturbance.

children.heading: Ratable reduction
children.label: 2 Ratable reduction
children.text: If the sum of the amount appropriated to carry out parts B and C, and part E of the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002 [ et seq.] for any fiscal year is less than $130,000,000, the amounts listed in paragraph (1) shall be ratably reduced for the fiscal year.20 U.S.C. 9567
